dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal describes two entry conditions: (1) Gap up above yesterday's high AND does not trade back down to that high - expressed as open > prev_high AND low > prev_high; (2) Lapse - opens above previous close and remains above - would require intra-bar data tracking. Primary condition uses first interpretation. Exit condition inferred as close below open (reversal) since AUTHOR_SILENT. Tested on 30-min S&P bars, Jan 2000-May 2011, 1600+ trades, avg $32/contract profit.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal describes two entry conditions: (1) Gap up above yesterday's high AND does not trade back down to that high - expressed as open > prev_high AND low > prev_high; (2) Lapse - opens above previous close and remains above - would require intra-bar data tracking. Primary condition uses first interpretation. Exit condition inferred as close below open (reversal) since AUTHOR_SILENT. Tested on 30-min S&P bars, Jan 2000-May 2011, 1600+ trades, avg $32/contract profit.

source_rule_text: Enter a trade when the market opens above yesterday's high and does not trade back down to that high (traditional gap), or when the market opens above the previous close and remains above that price (lapse).
